- '[[openalex-2605.14317-guided-diffusion-sampling-for-precipitation-forecast-interve]]'
title: NWP Validation of Interventions
---

**Background:** Numerical weather prediction (NWP) models require dynamically balanced initial conditions to produce reliable forecasts. Perturbations applied to atmospheric inputs in data-driven models may lack the physical consistency required for stable integration within an NWP system.

**Question / Future Work:** Determining whether intervention-based perturbations, generated by data-driven models, can successfully influence forecasts within an NWP framework remains an open challenge. Specifically, it is necessary to identify if such perturbations induce physically consistent, non-decaying changes when integrated through the dynamical cores of traditional weather models.

**Why It Matters:** Validating interventions against NWP models is critical for establishing the real-world operational feasibility of data-driven weather modification strategies.

**Evidence:** Simply adding generated perturbations does not ensure growth under NWP dynamics. Perturbations that are not dynamically balanced may rapidly decay during forecast integration.
